questions.question: Who coined the term 'biotechnology'?
questions.explanation: The term biotechnology was coined by Karl Ereky, a Hungarian scientist, in his book in 1917.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: In which year did Karl Ereky coin the term 'biotechnology'?
questions.explanation: The term biotechnology was coined by Karl Ereky... in 1917.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Ancient biotechnology includes the conservation of plant stocks by early farmers of which civilization?
questions.explanation: Right from the domestication of sheep and cattle... conservation of plant stocks by the early Egyptian farmers (ancient germplasm conservation)...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which yeast was accidentally discovered to cause the fermentation of unbaked dough?
questions.explanation: Fermented dough was discovered by accident when dough was not baked immediately and consequently it underwent fermentation by yeast such as Saccharomyces winlocki.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which civilization is credited with the discovery of Baker's Yeast, revolutionizing bread-making technology?
questions.explanation: In efforts to improve the technique, Baker's Yeast was discovered by the Romans, which revolutionised the bread-making technology prevalent then.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: In the process of curd making, which bacteria react with the milk protein casein?
questions.explanation: Lactobacillus bacteria from the curd react with the milk protein casein.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: What by-product of Lactobacillus denatures globular proteins to produce solid curds?
questions.explanation: Lactic acid formed as a by-product, denatures the globular proteins and coagulates to produce the solid curds...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Who established that yeast and other microbes were responsible for fermentation between the 1850s and 1860s?
questions.explanation: Between 1850s and 1860s, Louis Pasteur established that yeast and other microbes were responsible for fermentation.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: During World War I, industrial fermentation was established primarily because Germany required large amounts of which chemical for explosives?
questions.explanation: Industrial fermentation was established during World War I because of Germany's requirement for large amounts of glycerol for explosives.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: The invention of the modern fermenter (bioreactor) during World War II was catalyzed by the need for mass production of:
questions.explanation: World War II was the catalyst which led to the invention of the modern fermenter... for mass production of antibiotic penicillin.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Who made the first compound microscope in 1590?
questions.explanation: Thus with the advent of the first compound microscope, made by Dutch spectacle-maker Zacharias Janssen in 1590...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: In 1665, who examined thinly sliced cork and called the rectangular components 'cellulae'?
questions.explanation: In 1665, Robert Hooke, a physicist, examined thinly sliced cork and drew rectangular components, which he called cellulae...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Who concluded that 'all cells arise from pre-existing cells' in 1858?
questions.explanation: In 1858, Rudolf Virchow, a German pathologist, concluded that 'all cells arise from pre-existing cells'...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Who converted sugar to ethyl alcohol using yeast extracts, proving that biochemical transformations can occur without intact cells?
questions.explanation: In 1896 Eduard Buchner converted sugar to ethyl alcohol using yeast extracts, showing that biochemical transformations can occur without the use of cells.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which branch of biotechnology involves the application of biotechnology for marine and freshwater organisms?
questions.explanation: Blue Biotechnology: Application of biotechnology for marine and freshwater organisms...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: White biotechnology primarily deals with:
options: Improving industrial processes and enzyme usage
questions.explanation: White Biotechnology: Biotechnology applied to improve industrial processes and other production processes.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Dolly the sheep, the first mammal cloned from an adult somatic cell, eventually died due to:
questions.explanation: Dolly Sheep (5 Jul 1996-14 Feb 2003)... Died due to progressive lung disease.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which of the following organisms is widely used as a heterologous system for the commercial production of human insulin?
questions.explanation: At present, insulin is being produced predominantly in E. coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: The FDA has approved the production of a blood anti-coagulant for human use in the milk of which transgenic animal?
questions.explanation: For example, Food and Drug Administration, USA (FDA) has approved the production of blood anti-coagulant in milk of transgenic goats for human use.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Who performed the first, albeit unsuccessful, attempt at gene therapy in 1980?
questions.explanation: The first attempt, although unsuccessful, was performed by Martin Cline in 1980 for treating β-thalassemia.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: The first successful report of gene therapy in 1990 was used to treat which of the following disorders?
questions.explanation: The first successful report of gene therapy was achieved in 1990 when, Ashanthi De Silva was treated for Adenosine Deaminase deficiency [also called Adenosine Deaminase Severe Combined Immunodeficiency (ADA-SCID)]...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which country approved Gendicine in 2003 as the first commercial gene therapy product for cancer treatment?
questions.explanation: The first commercial gene therapy product approved for cancer treatment by China in 2003, was Gendicine.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: What is the toxic protein produced by Bacillus thuringiensis that acts as an insecticide?
questions.explanation: Bt is a toxic protein called Cry 1A(b), obtained from a soil bacterium called Bacillus thuringiensis...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Resistance against chilling was introduced into tobacco plants by introducing an enzyme gene from which plant?
questions.explanation: Among abiotic stress resistant plants, resistance against chilling has been introduced into tobacco plants by introducing gene for glycerol-1-phosphate acyl-transferase enzyme from Arabidopsis.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Roundup-ready soybeans are genetically modified to be unaffected by which specific herbicide?
questions.explanation: Similarly, Roundup-ready soybeans (Transgenic/GM soybeans) have been developed which are unaffected by the herbicide glyphosate...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: The transgenic crop 'Golden Rice' is nutritionally enhanced with high levels of:
questions.explanation: A classic example is the Golden Rice, which has high beta-carotene content (the precursor for vitamin A production in the human body)...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which of the following plants is NOT listed as a stock feed plant for expressing therapeutic molecules?
questions.explanation: Examples of such stock feed are bamboo, citronella, andropogon, foxtail millet, wheat grass, rice straw, etc.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Edible vaccines currently under rigorous clinical trials against measles and cholera are based on which transgenic plant?
questions.explanation: Potato based vaccines against measles, cholera, Norfolk virus, etc., are under rigorous clinical trials.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which crop uses very little water and is being investigated as a potential source of biofuel in arid conditions of Asia and Africa?
questions.explanation: In parts of Asia and Africa where drylands prevail, sweet sorghum is being investigated as a potential source of food, feed and fuel. Since the crop uses very little water, it is particularly suitable for growing in arid conditions.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which biofuel is often used as a direct replacement for gasoline?
questions.explanation: Bio-butanol, a biofuel, is often a direct replacement for gasoline.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Biodiesel is most commonly produced from oils or fats using which chemical process?
questions.explanation: Biodiesel is the most common biofuel in Europe, produced from oils or fats using trans-esterification.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which naturally occurring gene expressed in E. coli acts as a bio-sensor for detecting mercury contamination?
questions.explanation: For example, a reported gene, lux (which is responsible for emission of light), expressed in E. coli, acts as a bio sensor for detecting the mercury contamination.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: To degrade polychlorinated biphenyl (PCB), which degradation gene has been inserted into E. coli?
questions.explanation: An example of a degradation gene is biphenyl dioxygenase, which has been inserted in E.coli to degrade PCB (polychlorinated biphenyl).
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Hyper-accumulator plants like Brassica napus and Helianthus annus are used to remove which heavy metals from contaminated soils?
questions.explanation: Examples of some hyper-accumulators are Brassica napus and Helianthus annus, etc., for mercury and lead removal from contaminated soils.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: When was the National Biotechnology Board (NBTB) constituted by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: The National Biotechnology Board (NBTB) was constituted by Government of India in 1982...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: In which year was the National Biotechnology Board (NBTB) upgraded to the Department of Biotechnology (DBT)?
questions.explanation: ...which was subsequently upgraded to Department of Biotechnology (DBT) in 1986.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which of the following institutes established under DBT is located in Hyderabad, Telangana?
options: Centre for DNA Fingerprinting and Diagnostics (CDFD)
questions.explanation: Table 1.2: 1. Centre for DNA Fingerprinting and Diagnostics (CDFD), Hyderabad, Telangana
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: The National Institute for Plant Genome Research (NIPGR) is located in which city?
questions.explanation: Table 1.2: 7. National Institute for Plant Genome Research (NIPGR), New Delhi
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: India currently holds which rank among the top biotechnology powers in the world?
questions.explanation: Presently, India is among top 12 biotechnology powers in the world and third biggest industry in Asia Pacific...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: India has the world's largest production of which recombinant vaccine?
questions.explanation: India is world's largest producer of recombinant Hepatitis B vaccine.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which Indian company commercially launched the first ROTAVAC vaccine to eradicate rotavirus diarrhoea?
questions.explanation: Bharat Biotech commercially launched the first ROTAVAC vaccine to eradicate rotavirus diarrhoea in India.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which organization launched India's first MMR vaccine, 'Tresivac'?
questions.explanation: World's only adsorbed liquid HDC rabies vaccine and India's first MMR vaccine Tresivac' has been launched by the Serum Institute.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which biotech park is located in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Table 1.4: Biotechnology Park, Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: India's first indigenous Covid-19 Vaccine, Covaxin, was developed by which company?
questions.explanation: Covaxin, India's first indigenous Covid-19 Vaccine, was developed by a Biotechnology Company, Bharat Biotech International Ltd. Hyderabad...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: India is the world's largest producer of Statin and which other type of therapeutics?
questions.explanation: India is the world's largest producer of Statin and Immunosuppressants.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which reusable insulin delivery device was launched in India in 2015?
questions.explanation: In 2015, launched INSUPEN, a convenient and affordable reusable insulin delivery device.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: What percentage of the antiretroviral drugs for AIDS globally are provided by Indian pharmaceutical firms?
questions.explanation: 80 per cent of the antiretroviral drugs for AIDS are provided by Indian pharmaceutical firms globally.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: In the Indian hybrid seed industry, which genetically modified crop leads the market accounting for a 45% share?
questions.explanation: Since then Bt cotton is leading the market, accounting for 45% share in Indian hybrid seed industry.
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Samba Mahsuri is a genetically modified variety of rice known for being:
questions.explanation: Among these GM rice is the Blight resistant rice, Samba Mahsuri (developed through marker assisted backcrossing)...
questions.subject: Biotechnology

questions.question: Which sector represents an area of significant promise for India due to a huge skilled labour force and includes global contract research organizations like Quintiles?
questions.explanation: Bio-services sector represent an area of significant promise for India because of a huge skilled labour force... It includes global contract research organisations, such as Quintiles...
questions.subject: Biotechnology
